thesis_hint: This cycle should explore a new concept, not exploit an old one. Recent journal history is still exploit-heavy (about 6 of the last 10 entries), 1h dominates 8 of the last 10, and the recent explore work already touched AVNT/CELO reclaim-continuation shapes. Rotate again: target VIRTUAL 4h and test a negative-funding pullback-resumption concept built around volatility compression plus breakout hold above a medium trend anchor, with confirmation from expanding directional strength after the break. This is not a washout-reclaim, not a snapback fade, and not ETH failed-continuation inversion; the core question is whether mildly crowded 4h trend assets reward continuation after compressed pullbacks better than they reward reversal logic.
stop_condition: Stop when any variant reaches QScore >= 1.0 with profit_factor >= 1.12, total_trades >= 18, and max_drawdown_pct <= 9.0; abort any variant with total_trades < 12, profit_factor < 1.02, or trending regime profit_factor < 1.00.
rotation_reason: Force exploration this cycle: 1h work still dominates the last 10 journal entries, exploit loops still outweigh explore cycles, and only ETH channel currently has a walk-forward pass while rotation rules require a category change away from the overused 1h path.
